rules.title: Fortify (Passive)
rules.desc: When you would activate a unit, you can instead choose to fortify that unit. A unit can't activate and fortify. Place a fortify token next to that unit if it does not already have one. Once per round, you may activate a unit with a fortify token without spending an activation, consuming the fortify token. When a unit with fortify activates, it can't move more than 2”. If a unit activates normally, it loses its token.

rules.title: Mark (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Choose an enemy unit, and place a mark token on that unit. After a unit has had a mark token for 1 turn, attacks against that unit ignore obscuring terrain, and ranged attacks against that unit have their range increased by 3". The token is not removed until the unit finishes a movement 3" closer to that player's far board edge.

rules.title: Hold Ground (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Choose an enemy unit that is attempting to move within 1” of an allied unit. That unit can either choose to not enter within 1” of that unit, or that unit can trigger an action, however they can’t move during that action, and any attacks can only target the triggering unit. Ranged attacks made this way don’t suffer from inaccurate.

rules.title: Dig Out (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Chose a piece of terrain within 1" of an allied unit. Remove that piece of terrain from the board.

actions.name: Ironclad
actions.text: When wounds are allocated to Dwarf Guard, reduce the total wounds by 1d3.

actions.name: Terrify
actions.text: If an enemy unit is within 1” of Beastman, it can't target any other unit with attacks.

actions.name: Move Earth
actions.text: Move 6", Attack 6 (6"), Inflict 12 (6", move target unit up to 2")

actions.name: Disturb Earth
actions.text: Move 6", Place book within 6", All enemy units are Slowed immediately when they start or enter within 3" of the book. The book has toughness 6 and shares Dwarf Wizard’s wounds.

units.notes: Choose bow, or 2 of sword, hammer, shield, and axe
units.name: Dwarf Ranger

actions.name: Call to Arms
actions.text: If movement is taken directly towards an objective an enemy is on, and can score from, increase that movement by 3”

actions.name: Sudden Charge
actions.text: When Dwarf Cavalier activates from expending a fortify token, its attacks gain piercing, and its movement isn’t restricted from fortify.

actions.name: Axe
actions.text: Move 5”, Attack 5 (1”), Inflict 10 (1”, push a unit 2" in a direction)

actions.name: Hammer
actions.text: Move 5”, Attack 3 (1”, piercing) Inflict 6 (1”, piercing, push unit 2” in a direction)

actions.name: Defend the King
actions.text: The first attack any other allied unit makes against an enemy within 3" of Dwarf King each round adds 6 attack (no modifiers).

rules.title: Enrage (Passive)
rules.desc: Whenever a unit takes damage, place an enrage token on that unit if it does not already have one. At the end of your turn, remove all enrage tokens from all units. During your turn, when you activate a unit with an enrage token, it can move an additional 1d3”. If the unit attacks, it may reroll each attack die once.

rules.title: Prod (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Choose a unit within 3” of an allied unit. That unit receives 1 wound.

rules.title: Thrash (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Choose an allied unit with an enrage token. Remove the token. That unit is able to be activated an additional time this turn, with normal activation cost.

rules.title: Stampede (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Choose an enraged unit. When that unit moves this turn, all enemy units with equal or lower points cost that would be in the path of the movement are moved to the closest legal position to allow the movement. If a unit is not pushed, it can still be ignored when moving, but the stampede unit can't end its movement overlapping with a unit it can't push. This movement also ignores engagement.

actions.name: Cry For Help
actions.text: When Lizard Warrior receives an Enrage token, it can be immediately moved to any other unit within 6".

actions.name: Bonespeak
actions.text: Move 6", Choose a dead allied unit, place all models on battlefield within 1", that unit takes 1 action, remove that unit

actions.name: Carry
actions.text: Move 8" (flying), Attack 4 (1"), Inflict 4 (1", place all models within 1" of Lizard Birdrider at end of activation), Move 4" (flying)

actions.name: Spray
actions.text: Move 4", Attack 7 (6", melee, up to 2 targets)

actions.name: Territorial
actions.text: When activating, note the closest enemy unit within 10". Movement directly towards that unit is increased by 2”, and attacks against it are increased by 3 (before modifiers).

actions.name: Bloodlust
actions.text: When an enemy unit within 1” of Lizard Brute dies, it can immediately move 3”, then attack 2 (1”).

actions.name: Into the Fray
actions.text: If Lizard Outrider attacks a unit that has already been attacked this turn, its attacks gain piercing.

actions.name: Command
actions.text: Move 6", Choose a unit within 3", it gains an enrage token

actions.name: Battle Chant
actions.text: The first time during a turn a lizard within 3" would be killed, it instead lives with 1 remaining wound.

rules.title: Sticky (Passive)
rules.desc: Any enemy unit that starts movement while within 1” is slowed until the end of the turn.

rules.title: Leap Frog (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Choose an allied unit. When any other allied unit moves within 1” of that unit until the end of the turn, that move immediately becomes flying and is increased by 2d3”.

rules.title: Tongue Grab (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Choose an allied unit. A different unit within 5” of that unit that has equal or less remaining wounds is pulled up to 5” directly towards the chosen unit.

rules.title: Special Toxin (Manoeuver)
rules.desc: Choose an allied unit immediately before it attacks. That unit’s attack adds Inflict 10 (stun).

actions.name: Frog Splat
actions.text: Each time a frog warrior dies, reduce the remaining wounds that would be dealt to the unit by 2

actions.name: Poison Cloud
actions.text: Move 4", Inflict 10 (3", melee, all targets, staggered)

actions.name: Air Tackle
actions.text: Move 4 + 2d3", Attack 5 (1", piercing), Frog Elite unit immediately receives 1d3 wounds.

actions.name: Skewer
actions.text: Move 7" (flying), Attack 4 (1", piercing), Inflict 2 (1", piercing, execute)

actions.name: Snack
actions.text: Move 5", Inflict 8 (5", execute), heal 2d3 wounds on successful inflict

actions.name: Herd
actions.text: Move 5", 1 shroom unit within 6" can rush

actions.name: Charge
actions.text: Move 5", attack 8 (6"), 1 shroom unit within 6" can strike against the same target

actions.name: Heal
actions.text: Move 6", Heal 2d3 wounds from any unit within 3"

actions.name: Mighty Croak
actions.text: Move 6 + 1d3", Two units within 6" heal 1d3 wounds, then move up to 3"

rules.title: Nimble (Passive)
rules.desc: At the end of each unit's turn, if it didn't use all available movement, that unit can make an additional move equal to the unused movement, rounded down to the nearest inch.

rules.title: Misdirection (Maneuver)
rules.desc: Choose a unit that was chosen as the target of the attack. That attack must target a different unit of your choice within 3" of the original unit. The attack still uses the range and cover of the original unit, but the toughness of the new unit.

rules.title: Overgrowth (Maneuver)
rules.desc: Choose an objective. Until the start of your next turn, the objective area counts as obscuring.

rules.title: Hideaway (Maneuver)
rules.desc: Choose a unit within 1" of an obstacle. That unit is taken off the board. The next time that unit activates, it can be placed with all models within 1" of that obstacle at the start of its activation.

actions.name: Bush Brawl
actions.text: Melee attacks against models in this unit can be affected by obscuring cover.

actions.name: Spin
actions.text: Move 6" + Attack 3 (3", melee, cleave) against all enemy units within range at one point during the move

actions.name: Prickle
actions.text: Whenever a unit attacks Hogrider while within 1", Attack 3 (1") against that unit.

actions.name: Feed
actions.text: Move 6", Allied unit within 3" heals 1d3 wounds

actions.name: Hitch A Ride
actions.text: If any allied units with equal or less remaining wounds come within 1" of Gnome Sled during its movement, they can immediately move up to 5" directly towards Gnome Sled.

actions.name: Mighty Charge
actions.text: On the turn Gnome Mouserider is deployed, it can attack, and move 6" further.

rules.title: Empty The Warrens (passive)
rules.desc: At the end of your turn, choose up to 2 units either in reserve, or within 6" of your board edge. Those units can each move 4".

rules.title: Horde Rush (maneuver)
rules.desc: You can spend an activation to activate 2 units instead of 1, but both units must have a points cost of 1.

rules.title: Throw Rocks (maneuver)
rules.desc: Choose an enemy unit. All allied units within 7" of that unit can Attack 3 (7") against that unit.

rules.title: Taunt (maneuver)
rules.desc: Choose an allied unit. Until the start of your next turn, if any enemy attacks a different allied unit while the chosen unit is within range and line of sight of the attack, the attack is inaccurate.

units.notes: Each individual model chooses 2 wargear options, and each unit can have up to 2 of each wargear.
units.name: Kobold Grunt

actions.name: Shield
actions.text: Move 5", Attack 1 (1", piercing), Inflict 1 (1", piercing, stunned)

actions.name: Lots Of Stuff
actions.text: When choosing an action, if at least 1 model in the unit has a wargear option, that action can be chosen as if all models had that wargear option. If the wargear was chosen twice in the unit, roll as if there were an additional model in the unit.

actions.name: Unstack
actions.text: Move 6 + 1d3", Kobold Stack becomes Vulnerable until the end of your next turn

actions.name: Smell Blood
actions.text: When Kobold Foxrider moves directly towards an enemy unit that has been attacked this turn, its movement increases by 3", and its attack increases by 2.

actions.name: Inferno
actions.text: Move 5", Attack 6 (5", any number of targets), Inflict 6 (5", vulnerable, any number of targets)

actions.name: Fire Rush
actions.text: Move 5 + Xd3", Receive Xd3 wounds (roll separately)

actions.name: Duel
actions.text: Kobold Champion has 6 toughness against all units except the closest enemy unit to Kobold Champion, or any units within 1".

actions.name: Cutpurse
actions.text: Whenever an enemy unit within 1" dies or becomes inflicted, Kobold Thief can immediately Move 4" and Inflict 9 (1", disarmed)

gameRules.title: Dice
gameRules.body: All dice used in this game are either 3-sided (d3) or 6-sided (d6). When rolling a d3, roll a d6 and divide the result by 2, rounding up. On any die roll, a natural 1 is always a failure and a natural 6 is always a success, regardless of any modifiers applied.

gameRules.title: Building Your Army
gameRules.body:
Each player has 15 points to spend assembling their army. Each model has the following characteristics:

**Toughness** - The minimum roll required for an attack to wound this model.
**Wounds** - The number of successful hits required to destroy this model.
**Actions** - Abilities a model may use when its unit is activated. When you choose to activate a model, pick 1 of these.
**Passives** - Abilities that are always active, requiring no activation.

gameRules.title: Board Setup
gameRules.body:
The game is played on a central play area of 24" × 24". The full play surface may extend up to 36" × 36", but all deployment, objectives, and game rules refer to the central play area only. The extended area is just extra space for units to move. Far board edges refer to the 2 board edges of the 24" area that players will deploy from.

Place 2 central objectives on the centreline of the board, at least 10" apart. Then place 2 far objectives on each side, on a line 6" from each far board edge, also at least 10" apart. No objectives should be within 7" of any other objective (such that there is a 3" gap between all objective zones).

gameRules.title: Terrain
gameRules.body:
All pieces of terrain fall into 1 of 3 categories, and 1 of 3 cover traits. The terrain categories determine movement, and are as follows:

**Area** - Don't effect model movement
**Hazard** - Models can't end movement within a hazard, but can move through by spending an additional 2" of movement
**Obstacles** - Blocks model movement completely

Each of these will be combined with a cover trait. They are as follows:

**Open** - Provides no cover
**Obscuring** - Causes attacks that draw line of sight through this terrain to become inaccurate
**Blocking** - Prevents line of sight from being drawn

If a unit is within an obscuring area, or base to base with an obscuring obstacle, it can ignore that piece of terrain for the purpose of making attacks.

Both players agree on terrain placement before a game begins. Between 10 and 15 pieces of terrain should be placed. Each Hazard and Obstacle should be at least 3" from other Hazards and Obstacles, and no terrain should cover the area of any objective.

gameRules.title: Turns
gameRules.body:
At the start of the game, players roll off. The player who wins can either choose a board edge, or a turn order, giving the other decision to the player who lost the roll off.

On a player's turn, they can activate up to 4 different units. On the first turn of the player who acts first, they activate only 2 units. To activate a unit, if it is not on the battlefield, first place it on the battlefield with all models behind your board edge. You may then choose an action for that unit, and resolve that action. A unit can only be activated once per turn. On the turn a unit is first placed on the battlefield, it can't attack.

Some rules allow a unit to trigger, which allows them to take an action without spending one of the 4 activations for that turn. If a unit triggers during your turn, it cannot be activated later, and if a unit triggers during your opponent's turn, it becomes stunned. A unit cannot activate or trigger if it has already done so this turn.

gameRules.title: Manoeuvers
gameRules.body: At the beginning of each turn, that player gains 1 manoeuvre point. Each army has a list of manoeuvres they can access. During your turn, you can activate manoeuvres at any point. You can activate manoeuvres outside of your turn if the manoeuvre specifies. Each manoeuvre costs 1 point to activate, and the same manoeuvre can't be used more than once each turn.

gameRules.title: Scoring
gameRules.body:
At the start of each player's turn from their 3rd turn onwards, they may score from objectives. For each central objective, they score 1 point if they have more units within 2" of those objectives. If unit numbers are equal, they score points if they have greater points cost of units within 2". For each far objective (only the objectives furthest from a player's deployment), they score 1 point if they have any units within 2" of the objectives. No points can be scored from the objectives closest to a player's deployment.

The first player to 5 points wins.

gameRules.title: Movement
gameRules.body:
When a unit moves, each model may move up to the distance stated. Models may not move through other models. Each model must remain within 0.5" of at least one other model in its unit at all times during movement. If movement is Flying, the model may ignore terrain and allied models while moving, but must still end in a valid location.

If, during a move, any model in a unit comes within 1" of an enemy unit it was not previously within 1" of, that unit becomes engaged. For the remainder of that movement action, each model may only move directly towards the engaged enemy unit by the shortest legal path. Models may choose to stop at 1" and not close further, but may not move in any other direction for the rest of that move.

If a unit later moves again while already engaged, this restriction does not apply and it may move freely.

gameRules.title: Attacking
gameRules.body:
When a unit takes an action that calls for an attack, choose a valid target unit within range, then roll a number of dice equal to the attack's value for each model. Each die that equals or exceeds the target's Toughness inflicts 1 wound.

Attacks with a range of 1" are melee attacks by default, and attacks with a range of greater than 1" are ranged, unless otherwise specified. Ranged attacks may target any unit within the stated range, but only models within range may contribute to the attack. Melee attacks can be drawn from any point on a model, or any point on a different model within 0.5" in the same unit.

A unit's ranged attacks become Inaccurate if that unit has been within 1" of an enemy unit at any point during its current activation. Ranged attacks targeting a unit that is currently engaged in melee are also Inaccurate.

The following modifiers can be applied to attacks:

**Piercing** - The target's toughness is treated as 2 lower
**Cleave** - The attack rolls twice as many dice if the unit contains 2 or more models
**Inaccurate** - The attack rolls half of the number of dice, rounded up

Some attacks are labelled **Inflict** rather than Attack. An Inflict attack does not deal damage. Instead, if the attack would generate enough wounds to destroy the target model, the stated condition is applied to that unit, and a relevant token is applied. Unless otherwise specified, tokens are removed at the end of that player's turn. Below are common conditions:

**Stunned** - The unit can't activate, and doesn't limit movement of enemy units within 1"
**Disarmed** - The unit can't make attacks or inflict, and doesn't limit movement of enemy units within 1"
**Snared** - The unit can't move
**Slowed** - The unit can't move more than 3" total during its turn
**Staggered** - The unit's attacks have inaccurate
**Vulnerable** - Each successful hit from an attack applies 2 wounds instead of 1
**Execute** - The unit is killed

gameRules.title: Line Of Sight
gameRules.body:
Line of sight is drawn from any point on an attacking model's base to any point on the defending unit's base. The attacker chooses both endpoints.

Each model in the attacking unit draws its own line of sight independently. If a model cannot draw any unblocked line of sight to the target, it cannot participate in the attack. If a model's line of sight passes through blocking terrain, it is excluded. A model may still attack through obscuring terrain, but this imposes the Inaccurate modifier on the whole attack.

When resolving a ranged attack, the worst terrain penalty among all participating attacking models applies to the whole attack.
